summary: Red‑hot second set with The Other One, New Potato & Alligator
blurb: The February 14 1968 Carousel show delivers a blistering second set highlighted by The Other One suite and a soaring Dark Star > China Cat > The Eleven sequence. Jerry’s guitar is described as “wow‑fucking‑cool,” while Pigpen’s organ drives the Alligator jam. Soundboard sources capture the performance with crisp, crystal‑clear fidelity.
review: On February 14 1968 at San Francisco’s Carousel Ballroom the Grateful Dead served up one of their most electric mid‑’60s outings. The first set opens with a haunting Morning Dew and rolls straight into a seamless Dark Star > China Cat > The Eleven medley that still feels fresh in every listening. But it’s the second set that cements this night in legend – a relentless cascade of Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One → Cryptical Envelopment, followed by New Potato Caboose, Born Cross‑Eyed, Spanish Jam and a double‑Alligator sandwich punctuated by Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) and Feedback. Reviewers repeatedly call Jerry’s lead work “perfect tone,” “blazing fretboard” and outright “wow‑fucking‑cool,” especially during the Dark Star and The Other One explorations. Pigpen’s organ is front‑and‑center, adding texture to Alligator and powering the closing of The Other One suite. The soundboard captures are praised as “crisp,” “clear” and “legendary,” making this a must‑listen for anyone chasing that raw 1968 energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All seven recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the band’s tight flow, standout jams (The Other One suite, New Potato, Alligator) and exceptional individual performances (Jerry’s guitar, Pigpen’s organ). No negative commentary appears, just consistent high praise across sources.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality FM mix that captures the famed second‑set run with clarity; consistently high star scores and praised for its listenability.

key_highlights: Red‑hot run of The Other One suite and New Potato Caboose in the second set.
Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the Dark Star > China Cat > The Eleven sequence.
Jerry’s guitar described as “wow‑fucking‑cool” throughout, especially on Dark Star and The Other One.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is hailed for perfect tone, blazing fretboard work and an “in‑the‑zone” presence that drives Dark Star and the The Other One suite; reviewers call his playing "wow fucking cool."
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides solid, supportive bass with occasional improvisational space (notably on New Potato), though one reviewer notes a slight flatness in tuning.
band_performance.Pigpen: Pigpen’s organ is highlighted as essential to Alligator, The Other One suite and the overall psychedelic texture; several reviews praise its clarity and prominence.
